$comment: Ground-truth seeded defects for the api-docs eval. inject.ps1 applies every mutation's find->replace into a corrupted COPY of the fixture (never the original, never under docs/). score.ps1 matches reviewer/linter findings against the expected (file, docId, class) tuples. 'detector':'lint' classes are caught by the deterministic linter; 'detector':'llm' classes require an LLM reviewer. Each mutation's 'find' must occur exactly once in its fixture so injection is unambiguous.

mutations.find: <returns>The width of the text, in pixels.</returns>
mutations.replace: <returns>The the width of the text, in pixels.</returns>

mutations.find: A new <see cref="T:Sample.SampleColor" /> with the supplied alpha.
mutations.replace: A new <see cref="Sample.SampleColor" /> with the supplied alpha.

mutations.find: <summary>Draws the widget onto the current surface using the supplied font.</summary>
<summary>Gets or sets the width of the widget.</summary>

mutations.replace: <param name="text">The text to measure. See <xref:Sample.SampleWidget.Width> for details.</param>

mutations.find: The width, in pixels. The default value is 1.
mutations.replace: The width, in pixels. The default value is 100.
mutations.truth: The C# signature default and source initialize Width to 1; 100 is wrong.
The struct documents ARGB byte order; RGBA contradicts the example bytes 0xFF,0x80,0x40,0x20 -> A,R,G,B.

mutations.find: The original color is not modified because the value is a structure.
mutations.replace: The original color is not modified because the value is a structure. For an in-place update call <see cref="M:Sample.SampleColor.SetAlpha(System.Byte)" />.
mutations.truth: SampleColor exposes no SetAlpha member; the cross-reference is fabricated.
